bill.short_title: Further Additional Continuing Appropriations and Other Extensions Act, 2024

provisions.rate: at a rate for operations as provided in the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 (division A of Public Law 118-15), as further amended
provisions.raw_text: (1) by striking the date specified in section 106(3) and inserting ''March 8, 2024''; (2) by striking the date specified in section 106(4) and inserti
notes: Paragraph (1) extends the CR end date in section 106(3) to March 8, 2024
Paragraph (2) extends the date in section 106(4) to March 1, 2024
This is a further extension of the existing CR (P.L. 118-15, Division A), which was previously extended by the Further Continuing Appropriations and Other Extensions Act, 2024 (P.L. 118-22)

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 106(3)
cross_references.description: Extends CR end date to March 8, 2024

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 106(4)
P.L. 118-15, Division A

provisions.description: Amends section 123 of the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 by changing '94 days' to '129 days' and '94-day' to '129-day', extending the duration of the authority or limitation specified in that section to align with the extended CR period.
provisions.raw_text: (3) in section 123, by striking ''94 days'' and inserting ''129 days'' and by striking ''94-day'' and inserting ''129-day'';
notes: Modifies section 123 of P.L. 118-15, Division A to extend the day count from 94 to 129 days, consistent with the overall CR extension to March 8, 2024

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A, Section 123
cross_references.description: Extends day count from 94 to 129 days

(4) in section 124, by striking ''$663,070,000'' and inserting ''$2,199,260,000''; and

notes: Amends section 124 of P.L. 118-15 Division A, which was an anomaly in the original CR
Increases the dollar amount from $663,070,000 to $2,199,260,000, a net increase of $1,536,190,000
The specific account modified by section 124 is not named in this bill text; section 124 of the original CR was an anomaly provision

cross_references.description: Increases CR anomaly amount from $663,070,000 to $2,199,260,000

provisions.description: Allows amounts for NNSA Weapons Activities to be apportioned at a higher rate to mitigate issuing WARN notices for the Uranium Processing Facility (06-D-141) at Y-12, with a ceiling of $760,000,000.

metadata.account_name: Department of Energy—Atomic Energy Defense Activities—National Nuclear Security Administration—Weapons Activities

provisions.raw_text: SEC. 147. (a) Amounts made available by section 101 for ''Department of Energy—Atomic Energy Defense Activities—National Nuclear Security Administratio
notes: Added as new section 147 of P.L. 118-15 Division A by paragraph (5) of SEC. 101 of this bill
The $760,000,000 is a ceiling on the apportionment rate, not new budget authority above the CR rate
Purpose is to avoid Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) Act layoff notices for the Uranium Processing Facility project at Y-12

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A
cross_references.description: Adds new section 147 to the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024

provisions.description: Requires the Director of OMB to notify the Appropriations Committees within 3 days of each use of the SEC. 147(a) apportionment authority for NNSA Weapons Activities, and requires the Secretary of Energy to provide weekly reports specifying obligations incurred for the Uranium Processing Facility.
deadlines: 3 days after each use of the authority
provisions.raw_text: (b) The Director of the Office of Management and Budget shall notify the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate n
notes: This is subsection (b) of the new section 147 added to P.L. 118-15 Division A by paragraph (5)
Requires both OMB notification (within 3 days per use) and DOE weekly reporting on obligations

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A
cross_references.description: Part of new section 147 added to the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024

provisions.description: Allows amounts for FAA Operations to be apportioned at a higher rate necessary to fund mandatory pay increases and other inflationary adjustments, maintain and improve air traffic services, hire and train air traffic controllers, and continue aviation safety oversight, while avoiding service reductions.
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 148. Amounts made available by section 101 for ''Department of Transportation—Federal Aviation Administration—Operations'' may be apportioned up t
notes: Added as new section 148 of P.L. 118-15 Division A by paragraph (5) of SEC. 101 of this bill
No specific dollar ceiling is provided, unlike SEC. 147 which caps at $760M
Purpose is to maintain FAA operations including air traffic control hiring/training and aviation safety oversight

cross_references.target: P.L. 118-15, Division A
cross_references.description: Adds new section 148 to the Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024

provisions.description: Exempts the budgetary effects of Division B from statutory PAYGO scorecards, Senate PAYGO scorecards, and budget scorekeeping for purposes of discretionary spending limits, 302(a) allocations, and Statutory PAYGO Act classifications.
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text: The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go
notes: Standard budget enforcement exemption provision. Subsection (a) exempts from statutory PAYGO, (b) from Senate PAYGO, (c) from BCA section 251 caps, 302(a) allocations, and Statutory PAYGO Act appropriation classification.

cross_references.target: Section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010
Section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress)
Section 251 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985
Section 302(a) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974

flagged_issues: SEC. 101(4) modifies section 124 of P.L. 118-15 but the specific account name is not stated in this bill text; confidence lowered accordingly
SEC. 148 provides an apportionment anomaly for FAA Operations without a specific dollar ceiling, unlike SEC. 147 which caps at $760,000,000
All provisions in Division A amend the existing Continuing Appropriations Act, 2024 (P.L. 118-15, Division A); no new standalone appropriations are made
SEC. 122 reduces Medicaid Improvement Fund by $655,689,081 (from $5,796,117,810 to $5,140,428,729) as a budget offset
SEC. 132 reduces Medicare Improvement Fund by $53,000,000 (from $2,250,795,056 to $2,197,795,056) as a budget offset
All mandatory spending extensions in this division extend programs through approximately March 8-9, 2024, bridging from the prior CR expiration on January 19, 2024
No discretionary appropriations in Division B; all provisions are mandatory spending extensions, authorizing statute amendments, or policy riders
